county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Rusk County formed 1843 from Nacogdoches. County seat is Henderson. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Henderson. East Texas county. Rusk County Heritage Association. Depot Museum in Henderson. East Texas oil field heritage (1930s boom). Significant African American community. Check Nacogdoches for pre-1843 records. Freedmen's Bureau records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Rusk County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1843; County Clerk in Henderson holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: Rusk County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1843 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.source_name: Rusk County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1843. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: Rusk County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1843. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: Rusk County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Rusk County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Rusk County Enslaved Persons Records -- TSLAC / County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Estate inventories listing enslaved persons, bills of sale, tax rolls listing slaveholders and enslaved persons counts, and Freedmen's Bureau records (NARA). Rusk County was part of the Texas plantation economy.

source_profiles.source_name: Rusk County Oil and Gas Lease Records -- GLO / County Clerk
source_profiles.repository: Texas General Land Office, Austin / County Clerk, Henderson
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Oil and gas lease records, mineral rights transfers, and related land documents from 1930. Texas GLO maintains state mineral records. County Clerk has private mineral lease records.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- Rusk County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for Rusk County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Sabine County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Sabine). County seat is Hemphill. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Hemphill. Original Texas county (deep East Texas, Sabine River). Sabine County Historical Commission. McMahan's Chapel (1833) — one of the oldest Protestant churches in Texas. Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC. Timber heritage.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Sabine County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Hemphill holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for Sabine County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

county_metadata.parent_county: Original Texas county (Municipality of San Augustine)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: San Augustine County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of San Augustine). County seat is San Augustine. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in San Augustine. Original Texas county — one of the oldest Anglo-settled areas (1790s+). San Augustine County Historical Commission. Ezekiel Cullen House. El Camino Real heritage. San Augustine was called the 'Cradle of Texas.' Some of the earliest Protestant church records in Texas. Check for Spanish/Mexican era records at TSLAC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for San Augustine County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in San Augustine holds deed books

county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Coldspring. East Texas county. San Jacinto County Heritage Society. Sam Houston National Forest. Check Liberty, Montgomery, Polk, Walker for pre-1870 records. Timber heritage.

county_metadata.parent_county: Original Texas county (Municipality of San Patricio)
county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: San Patricio County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of San Patricio). County seat is Sinton. South Texas / Rio Grande Valley region with Spanish colonial and Mexican land grant heritage. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Sinton. Original Texas county with Irish colonial heritage (McGloin and McMullen's Irish colony, 1829). San Patricio County Museum. Catholic heritage (Irish settlers). Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC. Ranching and farming. Portland and Ingleside area.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for San Patricio County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Sinton holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: San Patricio County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1836 to present. Continuous coverage from county formation.

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1836. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: San Patricio County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1836. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: San Patricio County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1850 to 1950 for San Patricio County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Spanish Colonial / Mission Records -- San Patricio County Area
source_profiles.repository: Catholic Archives of San Antonio / TSLAC / Bexar Archives at UT-Austin
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Spanish colonial records including mission registers (baptisms, marriages, burials), presidio records, and land grants. San Patricio County area was part of the Spanish colonial system. Records at Catholic Archives of San Antonio, TSLAC, and Bexar Archives at UT-Austin.
source_profiles.survival_notes: Colonial-era records vary in survival; many originals at Catholic Archives or in Mexican archives

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- San Patricio County Land Grants
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Republic of Texas and early statehood land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for San Patricio County. GLO archives are online with searchable databases. Texas managed its own public lands (unique among states). Empresario colony records if applicable.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: San Saba County formed 1856 from Bexar. County seat is San Saba. Texas Hill Country region with German, Czech, and frontier heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1873 courthouse fire; some records destroyed.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in San Saba. 1873 courthouse fire destroyed some records. Central Texas county. San Saba County Museum. Spanish missions nearby (Santa Cruz de San Saba, 1757, destroyed by Comanche). Check Bexar for pre-1856 records. Pecan heritage ('Pecan Capital of the World').
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for San Saba County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1856; County Clerk in San Saba holds deed books

source_profiles.source_name: San Saba County Deed Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Deed books from 1873 to present. Some gaps due to 1873 courthouse fire; some records destroyed.

source_profiles.source_name: San Saba County Marriage Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Marriage licenses from 1873. Texas county clerks have maintained marriage records since county formation. State registration from 1966.

source_profiles.source_name: San Saba County Probate Records -- County Clerk
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Probate records, wills, estate inventories, and guardianships from 1873. Texas probate records are maintained at the county level by the County Clerk.

source_profiles.source_name: San Saba County Federal Census Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1856 to 1950 for San Saba County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.source_name: Native American Records -- San Saba County Area (Apache and Comanche)
source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for Apache and Comanche in the San Saba County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

source_profiles.source_name: Texas General Land Office -- San Saba County Land Records
source_profiles.coverage_notes: State land grants, patents, surveys, and field notes for San Saba County from 1856. GLO archives include searchable online databases.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Schleicher County formed 1887 from Crockett. County seat is Eldorado. West Texas / Permian Basin region with ranching, oil, and frontier heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Eldorado. West-central Texas county organized 1901. Schleicher County Historical Society. Ranching heritage. Check Crockett for pre-organization records.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Scurry County formed 1876 from Bexar. County seat is Snyder. Texas Panhandle / South Plains region with ranching and farming heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Snyder. Rolling Plains county organized 1884. Scurry County Museum on Western Texas College campus. Buffalo hunters' heritage (1870s). Oil discovery (Canyon Reef, 1948). Check Bexar for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Scurry County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1876; County Clerk in Snyder holds deed books

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Shackelford County formed 1858 from Bosque. County seat is Albany. Central Texas region with farming, ranching, and diverse immigrant heritage.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Albany. Central-west Texas county organized 1874. Fort Griffin (frontier military post) State Historic Site — essential for frontier era research. Shackelford County Historical Commission. Old Jail Art Center. The Fandangle outdoor pageant. Check Bosque for pre-organization records.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Shackelford County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1858; County Clerk in Albany holds deed books

source_profiles.coverage_notes: Federal census from 1858 to 1950 for Shackelford County. Texas first enumerated in federal census in 1850. Agricultural and manufacturing schedules available for select years.

source_profiles.repository: NARA, Fort Worth / Oklahoma Historical Society
source_profiles.coverage_notes: Pre-settlement and frontier-era records for various tribes in the Shackelford County area. Includes Indian Affairs records, military campaign records, and reservation records at NARA Fort Worth. Texas frontier counties experienced significant Native American conflict through the 1870s.

county_metadata.jurisdictional_notes: Shelby County formed 1836 from Original Texas county (Municipality of Tenaha). County seat is Center. East Texas / Piney Woods region with timber industry and plantation heritage. BURNED COUNTY: 1882 courthouse fire; early records lost. One of the original Texas counties from the Republic of Texas era.
county_metadata.research_tips: County Clerk in Center. 1882 courthouse fire destroyed early records — check TSLAC for backups. Original Texas county (East Texas). Shelby County Historical Commission. Shelby County was the center of the Regulator-Moderator War (1839-1844). Check for Republic-era records at TSLAC.
county_metadata.online_land_records_scope: Texas GLO online archives for Shelby County land grants, patents, and surveys from 1836; County Clerk in Center holds deed books
